title: EEG Signal Quality: Garbage In, Garbage Out
preview: Working on the EEG project made me realize that neuroscience is a field where signal processing directly matters. The data is messy, but the patterns are real if you clean it right.

detail.body:
Working on the EEG project made me realize that neuroscience is a genuinely interesting field, and that signal processing has a direct home in it. The brain produces real, measurable signals. The problem is that everything else does too.

A 60 Hz power line can completely drown out the alpha band. Eye blinks create spikes way larger than the neural signals we care about. Before we cleaned the data, our SVM hit 51% accuracy, which is barely above random guessing. After a proper pipeline including a notch filter at 60 Hz, a bandpass from 1 to 50 Hz, and ICA to remove eye artifacts, the same model jumped to 78%. The model was never the problem. The data quality was.

That gap, from 51% to 78%, just from preprocessing, is what made me appreciate how much engineering goes into science before any machine learning even starts.
